news.title: Who has most goals in World Cup history? Kylian Mbappé passes Lionel Messi with 2 goals in third-place game
news.lede: With the 2026 World Cup in full swing, it was only a matter of time before Argentina's Lionel Messi and France’s Kylian Mbappé broke the all-time record that stood before the tournament. Already considered by many as the greatest of all time, Messi was the first to score more goals than any other player in World Cup history. He earned his 17th and 18th during Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria. Then he added another against Jordan on a sublime free kick from outside the box for his 19th in the competition.

news.title: Messi stands alone in World Cup scoring history. This chart shows how
news.lede: When Lionel Messi takes the pitch for the 2026 World Cup final against Spain at 3 p.m. ET on July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, it might be his final international match for Argentina. But regardless of whether it is his final game or not, Messi will be far and away the all-time leader in goal contributions at the World Cup. USA TODAY analyzed goal and assist data from FIFA and Opta/Stats Perform to compile the 10 players with the most goal contributions in men’s World Cup history.

news.title: What time is Argentina vs. Spain game today? How to watch World Cup final
news.lede: The stage is set for the final showdown of the 2026 World Cup, with just the reigning champions, Argentina, and the reigning European champions, Spain, remaining. Spain, known as La Roja, is aiming for their second World Cup title, having previously won in 2010. Meanwhile, Lionel Scaloni's Argentine squad hopes to become only the third team in history to win back-to-back World Cups, with the quest coming to a head on Sunday, June 19 in the 2026 World Cup final. The match promises to be a clash of titans — a battle between an unstoppable force and an immovable object.

news.title: Arsenal's World Cup finalists
news.lede: It's European versus world champions in Sunday's World Cup final and Arsenal have three players in Luis de la Fuente's Spain side who face Argentina live on the BBC from 19:00 BST. Mikel Merino has been dubbed the World Cup 'super sub' after scoring two last-minute winners in the knockout stages to eliminate Portugal and Belgium from the competition. At this tournament, he has become the first player in World Cup history to score the winning goal in two different knockout-stage matches as a substitute, and also the first Spanish player to score two winning goals after the 80th minute at the World Cup. Goalkeeper David Raya and midfielder Martin Zubimendi have been named on the bench for every game of the competition, but are yet to feature.

news.title: Every team to have defended the World Cup as Argentina chase history
news.lede: Argentina will aim to make history this evening as the world champions look to secure consecutive World Cup titles. The World Cup is the biggest prize in football and winning successive editions is an almost impossible feat. Throughout the tournament’s near-century of history, only two teams have ever defended the World Cup. No nation has achieved the feat since 1962.

news.title: I've been a World Cup official - I promise you there is no favouritism
news.lede: Lionel Messi is set to line up for Argentina against Spain in Sunday's World Cup final. According to many fans, social media timelines and corners of the internet, Fifa wants Argentina and Lionel Messi to win the World Cup - and the officials are all in on it. As referees, we are forced to deal with the idea that we are part of some kind of conspiracy theory all the time. I can't stress this strongly enough.

news.title: World Cup final 2026: Is this the last time we'll see Argentina's Lionel Messi?
news.lede: Lionel Messi has scored 15 of his 21 World Cup goals in 2022 and 2026. Argentina are one win away from sporting immortality. Lionel Scaloni's side are bidding to become just the third team to win successive World Cups, after Italy (1934 and 1938) and Brazil (1958 and 1962). If they are to pip European champions Spain to the crown, they will need Lionel Messi to be at his best.

news.title: World Cup 2026 Latest Scores and Fixtures
news.lede: Spain will face Argentina in the World Cup final on Sunday, July 19 at 8pm UK time (3pm local time) from the New York New Jersey Stadium. When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Spain beat France to make it to their first World Cup final in 16 years, while Argentina are looking to retain the trophy they won four year's ago in Qatar. Early into the knock-outs, we saw a couple of big sides exit the tournament, with Germany and the Netherlands falling to Paraguay and Morocco respectively.
